_meta.description: Canonical taxonomy of 134 Excel calculation families spanning descriptive statistics, distributions, inferential statistics, regression, time series, finance, optimization, matrix math, and data cleaning.

families.description: Compute adjusted coefficient of determination accounting for number of predictors.
Generate a full amortization schedule with principal, interest, and balance per period.
Compute autocorrelation function (ACF) at specified lag(s) for a time series.
Compute probability or inverse for a beta distribution with given alpha and beta parameters.
Group continuous values into discrete bins using equal-width, equal-frequency, or custom breakpoints.
Compute binomial probability for k successes in n trials with probability p.
Price European call/put options using the Black-Scholes model.
Calculate the present value (price) of a bond given coupon rate, yield, and maturity.
Calculate yield to maturity (YTM) or current yield of a bond.
Calculate the break-even point where total revenue equals total cost.
Calculate the compound annual growth rate between a beginning and ending value.
Calculate expected return using CAPM: E(R) = Rf + β(Rm - Rf).
Compute chi-square probability density, CDF, or inverse for given degrees of freedom.
Test whether observed frequencies differ significantly from expected frequencies.
Test whether two categorical variables are independent using a contingency table.
Calculate the ratio of standard deviation to mean, expressing relative variability.
Compute confidence interval for a population mean given sample data and confidence level.
Compute pairwise correlation coefficients between multiple variables.
Compute covariance between two data sets (population or sample).
Compute the cross product of two 3D vectors.
Evaluate the CDF of a specified distribution at a given point.
Compute running cumulative sum across a data range.
Scale data to a standard range (e.g., 0-1) using min-max or other normalization methods.
Transform data to zero mean and unit variance using z-score standardization.
Convert data between types: text to number, number to date, etc.
Apply validation constraints to a range (e.g., numeric bounds, list, custom formula).

families.displayName_pt: Tabela de Dados de Uma Variável
families.description: Create a one-variable data table to analyze how changing one input affects results.
aliases_pt: tabela de dados de uma variável

families.displayName_pt: Tabela de Dados de Duas Variáveis
families.description: Create a two-variable data table to analyze how changing two inputs affects a result.
aliases_pt: tabela de dados de duas variáveis

families.description: Calculate depreciation using declining balance (DB) or double declining balance (DDB) method.
Identify and remove duplicate rows or values from a data range.
Calculate the time required to recover an investment using discounted cash flows.
Compute the dot (inner) product of two vectors.
Apply Holt's double exponential smoothing to capture level and trend in time series.
Calculate effect size metrics: Cohen's d, Hedge's g, or eta-squared.
Compute eigenvalues and eigenvectors of a square matrix.
Fit a regression model with combined L1 and L2 regularization.
Compute probability for an exponential distribution with given rate parameter.
Compute exponentially weighted moving average giving more weight to recent observations.
Compute probability for an F-distribution with given numerator and denominator degrees of freedom.
Forecast future values based on historical data using linear or ETS methods.
Compute frequency counts for data grouped into bins.
Calculate the future value of an investment with periodic payments and/or lump sum.
Compute probability for a gamma distribution with given shape and scale.
Find the input value that makes a formula reach a target result.
Calculate period-over-period growth rate (simple or logarithmic).
Run a general hypothesis test framework: define null/alternative, compute test statistic, and p-value.
Solve for the periodic interest rate given PV, FV, PMT, and NPER.
Calculate the IRR for a series of cash flows.
Estimate missing intermediate values using linear, polynomial, or spline interpolation.
Calculate the IQR (Q3 - Q1) as a robust measure of spread.
Compute the inverse CDF (quantile) for a given probability and distribution.
Non-parametric one-way ANOVA by ranks for comparing three or more independent groups.
Measure the tailedness of a distribution relative to a normal distribution.
Create lagged versions of a time series for correlation or regression analysis.
Fit a regression model with L1 regularization for variable selection.
Fit a logistic regression model for binary classification outcomes.
Compute probability for a log-normal distribution with given mean and standard deviation of ln(X).
Non-parametric test comparing two independent samples without assuming normality.
Compute the determinant of a square matrix.
Compute the multiplicative inverse of a square matrix.
Multiply two matrices with compatible dimensions.
Determine the rank (number of linearly independent rows/columns) of a matrix.
Sum the diagonal elements of a square matrix.
Transpose a matrix (swap rows and columns).
Calculate the arithmetic mean (average) of a data range.
Calculate the geometric mean, useful for rates of change and multiplicative processes.
Calculate the harmonic mean, useful for rates and ratios.
Calculate the mean after trimming a percentage of extreme values from both tails.
Calculate the weighted average using a separate weight range.
Find the middle value when data is sorted, robust to outliers.
Fill missing or blank cells using mean, median, mode, forward-fill, or interpolation.
Find the most frequently occurring value(s) in a data set.
Calculate MIRR using separate finance and reinvestment rates.
Run Monte Carlo simulation by sampling random inputs and computing output distribution.
Fit a linear model with multiple independent variables.
Calculate the NPV of a series of cash flows at a given discount rate.
Compute probability for a normal (Gaussian) distribution with given mean and standard deviation.
Solve for the number of periods (NPER) given rate, payment, PV, and FV.
Test whether the mean of a sample differs significantly from a known or hypothesized population mean.
Test whether means of three or more groups differ significantly.
Identify outliers using IQR fences, z-score thresholds, or modified z-scores.
Compute the p-value for a given test statistic and distribution.
Compare means of two related samples (before/after, matched pairs).
Calculate the time required to recover an initial investment from cash flows.
Calculate the periodic payment for a loan or annuity.
Compute Pearson's r linear correlation coefficient between two variables.
Calculate the k-th percentile of a data set.
Compare values between consecutive or specified periods (MoM, QoQ, YoY).
Compute probability for a Poisson distribution with given lambda (mean rate).
Fit a polynomial of specified degree to the data.
Calculate the variance and standard deviation of a weighted portfolio of assets.
Calculate prediction interval for a new observation from a regression model.
Calculate the present value of future cash flows discounted at a given rate.
Evaluate the PDF of a specified distribution at a given point.
Calculate Q1, Q2 (median), or Q3 of a data set.
Compute the coefficient of determination measuring goodness of fit.
Generate random samples from a specified distribution.
Calculate the range (max - min) of a data set.
Assign ranks or percentile ranks to values in a data set.
Compute residuals from a regression model and assess normality and patterns.
Fit a regression model with L2 regularization to handle multicollinearity.
Compute rolling (windowed) statistics: mean, stdev, min, max, sum over a sliding window.
Calculate the required sample size for a study given desired confidence, power, and effect size.
Define and compare base, best-case, and worst-case scenarios for a model.
Decompose a time series into trend, seasonal, and residual components.
Measure how changes in input variables affect a model's output (tornado chart data).
Calculate the Sharpe ratio measuring risk-adjusted return.
Fit a straight line (y = mx + b) to two-variable data.
Compute a simple (unweighted) moving average over a specified window.
Decompose a matrix into U, Σ, and V* components (SVD).
Measure the asymmetry of a distribution (left-skew vs right-skew).
Solve a linear optimization problem with constraints using the simplex method.
Solve a nonlinear optimization problem with constraints using GRG or evolutionary methods.
Calculate the Sortino ratio using downside deviation instead of total volatility.
Compute Spearman's rank correlation coefficient between two variables.
Calculate the standard deviation (sample or population) of a data set.
Calculate the statistical power (1 - β) for a given test, sample size, and effect size.
Calculate equal periodic depreciation using the straight-line method.
Calculate depreciation using the sum-of-years-digits method.
Generate a full descriptive summary: count, mean, median, stdev, min, max, quartiles, skewness, kurtosis.
Compute probability for a Student's t-distribution with given degrees of freedom.
Fit and extract the trend component from a time series (linear, polynomial, or exponential).
Trim or winsorize extreme values at specified percentile thresholds.
Compare means of two independent samples assuming equal variances.
Analyze the effect of two categorical factors and their interaction on a continuous outcome.
Compute probability for a continuous or discrete uniform distribution.
Calculate the variance (sample or population) of a data set.
Calculate WACC from equity cost, debt cost, tax rate, and capital structure.
Compute probability for a Weibull distribution with given shape and scale parameters.
Compute a moving average with custom weights for each period in the window.
Compare means of two independent samples without assuming equal variances.
Evaluate how changing one or more input values affects formula outcomes.
Non-parametric test for comparing two related samples using signed ranks.
Calculate the z-score (standard score) for each value in a data set.
Test a sample mean against a known population mean when population stdev is known.
